definition: Encode each sample location with a spatial representation model or handcrafted location features, then compute cosine similarity between evaluation and training location embeddings. Use this when raw distance is too coarse and geography interacts with task-specific covariates.
example_usage: python embedding_cosine_similarity.py --train train_geo.csv --eval eval_geo.csv --out pairwise_similarity.csv --embedding-cols-prefix loc_

citations.title: TorchSpatial: A Location Encoding Framework and Benchmark for Spatial Representation Learning

evidence.summary: TorchSpatial introduces a location encoding benchmark and metrics for geographic bias across geo-aware tasks.
The framework implements 2D and 3D location encoders, including methods that preserve spherical surface distance.
The paper connects model performance patterns to spatial autocorrelation and geographic bias.

limitations: Location embeddings may encode sampling bias and label priors rather than causal scientific mechanisms.
Embedding similarity is only interpretable if the encoder and training data are documented.
Different location encoders can produce different novelty orderings.
quality_gates: Report the location encoder and whether it was trained on evaluation labels or metadata.
Compare embedding similarity to raw geographic distance.
Audit performance by geographic region to detect systematic hot spots or cold spots.
